steps: $F(\mathbb{R}^2,2)$ is the complement of the diagonal $\Delta = \{(x,x)\}$ in $(\mathbb{R}^2)^2 \cong \mathbb{R}^4$.
The map $\phi: F(\mathbb{R}^2,2) \to \mathbb{R}^2 \setminus \{0\}$ given by $\phi(x_1,x_2) = x_2 - x_1$ is a homotopy equivalence. (It admits a fibre-wise deformation retraction via translation.)
The generator of $\pi_1 = H_1 = \mathbb{Z}$ is the loop where one point winds around the other: the pure braid group $P_2 = \pi_1(F(\mathbb{R}^2,2)) = \mathbb{Z}$.
More generally: $F(\mathbb{R}^2, n) \simeq \bigvee_{k=1}^{n-1} \bigvee^k S^1$ (homotopy equivalent to a wedge of circles), and $H_1(F(\mathbb{R}^2,n)) = \mathbb{Z}^{n(n-1)/2}$.
